metadata.summary: Microsoft's enterprise PM solution with four process templates, tight Azure stack integration, and AB# commit traceability. Use Inherited processes, pin one per org, skip customization.

content.title: Azure DevOps Boards
content.summary: Azure DevOps Boards is Microsoft's enterprise project management solution supporting four process templates (Basic, Agile, Scrum, CMMI) with tight integration to Azure Pipelines, Repos, and Test Plans. The critical constraint: use Inherited processes only, pin one process per organization, and tie commits to work items via AB#1234 in commit messages — this gives automatic traceability from requirement to build to release without third-party glue.

why.p: Microsoft-stack organizations gain the most from ADO Boards because Entra ID (AAD), Azure Pipelines, and Repos integrate at the identity layer — PAT scopes, area-path security, and pipeline environments all share the same permission model. The Analytics OData feed enables Power BI dashboards that WIQL cannot produce for multi-sprint trends without a separate data warehouse.

list.item: Microsoft-stack organizations already on Azure (Entra, Azure Pipelines, Repos, Test Plans).
Regulated industries (CMMI process template) needing audit trails on every state transition.
Teams wanting work-item to commit to build to release traceability without third-party glue.
Enterprise portfolios where Epic → Feature → Story → Task hierarchy maps to OKRs or quarterly planning.
Organizations standardizing on a single vendor to consolidate tool sprawl.
Pure SaaS or non-Microsoft ecosystem — ADO is heavyweight outside the Azure stack.
Small / fast-moving startups — Linear, Shortcut, or GitHub Projects feel significantly lighter.
Open-source projects with external contributors — onboarding to ADO creates friction.
Teams needing a modern mobile UX — ADO Boards trails Linear, Jira, and Shortcut on UI quality.
Heavy process customization beyond inheritance — XML "Hosted XML" is deprecated in cloud.

section.name: process-setup
section.p: Process template selection, work-item hierarchy design, board configuration, and area/iteration path setup.

rule.statement: Use Inherited processes only; never customize system fields; pin one process per organization across all projects.
rule.rationale: XML "Hosted XML" process customization is deprecated in ADO cloud. Mixing Agile and Scrum across projects breaks cross-project WIQL queries because work-item type names differ (User Story vs PBI).

list.item: Basic: Issue, Task — for simple non-engineering projects with minimal hierarchy.
Agile: Epic, Feature, User Story, Task, Bug — for agile software teams; most agent-friendly due to widespread training data.
Scrum: Epic, Feature, PBI, Task, Bug, Impediment — for Scrum teams; PBI instead of User Story.
CMMI: Epic, Feature, Requirement, Task, Bug, Change Request, Review, Risk — for regulated industries requiring full audit trail on every transition.
Epic: large initiative; business value and time criticality fields; maps to OKRs for portfolio reporting.
Feature: shippable capability within an Epic; must have acceptance criteria and story points.
User Story (or PBI): unit of user value; Gherkin-format acceptance criteria recommended.
Task: technical work item with remaining work hours for sprint burndown.
Bug: defect that can exist at any hierarchy level.

rule.statement: Tie commits to work items via "AB#1234" in commit message — ADO auto-links the commit, build, and release to the work item without any additional configuration.
rule.rationale: AB# linking is the only zero-configuration traceability mechanism in ADO. It creates the commit-to-work-item-to-build-to-release chain that justifies ADO over standalone PM tools.

rule.statement: Set up a "definition of ready" board column rule to block User Stories without Acceptance Criteria from entering the sprint; enforce this at the board, not in team norms.
rule.rationale: Board-level enforcement is applied universally; team norms are inconsistently applied under sprint pressure.

example.title: Board configuration with WIP limits
example.code: board_settings: columns: - name: "New" state_mapping: ["New"] - name: "Active" state_mapping: ["Active"] wip_limit: 5 split: true # Doing/Done split within column - name: "Resolved" state_mapping: ["Resolved"] wip_limit: 3 - name: "Closed" state_mapping: ["Closed"] swimlanes: - name: "Expedite" default: false - name: "Default" default: true card_styles: - name: "Blocked" condition: "Tags Contains 'Blocked'" background_color: "#FF0000"

rule.statement: Use iteration path strings exactly as configured — they are case-sensitive and use backslash separators; "Project/Sprint 1" and "Project\Sprint 1" are different paths.
rule.rationale: ADO iteration paths fail silently when the separator or casing is wrong, producing empty assignment results with no error message.

example.title: Iteration path with proper escaping
example.note: Always escape backslashes in code; use double backslash in strings.

section.name: queries-automation
section.p: WIQL query design, automation rule patterns, pipeline integration, and when to use Analytics OData instead of WIQL.

rule.statement: Use WIQL for live queries on current sprint and board state; use Analytics OData for any report spanning more than 2 sprints — WIQL is slow and times out on historical trend queries.
rule.rationale: WIQL executes against the live work-item store; it is optimized for current-state lookups. Analytics OData is a pre-aggregated columnar store designed for historical analysis and Power BI integration.

example.title: WIQL queries for live board state
example.code:
-- My active items (WIQL) SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.State] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.AssignedTo] = @Me AND [System.State] IN ('New', 'Active') ORDER BY [Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority]
-- Current sprint burndown (WIQL) SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.RemainingWork] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.IterationPath] = @CurrentIteration AND [System.WorkItemType] IN ('User Story', 'Bug', 'Task')
-- Features without child stories (WIQL) SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.WorkItemType] = 'Feature' AND [System.State] <> 'Closed' AND NOT [System.Id] IN ( SELECT [System.Parent] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.WorkItemType] = 'User Story' )

antipattern.description: WIQL WAS queries on large issue sets for historical analysis.
antipattern.reason: WAS queries on transition history time out above approximately 10,000 issues. Use Analytics WorkItemRevisions entity via OData for historical state analysis.

rule.statement: Write automation rules that set the iteration path on activation only when the field is currently empty; unconditional iteration-set rules override manual assignments.
rule.rationale: Unconditional iteration assignment on state change silently moves manually-placed items to the current sprint, losing planned placement.

example.title: Safe automation rule patterns
example.code:
rules: - name: "Auto-close children when parent done" trigger: child_state_changed conditions: - all_children_state: "Closed" actions: - set_state: "Closed"
- name: "Set iteration on first activation" trigger: state_changed_to_active conditions: - iteration_is_empty: true actions: - set_iteration: "@CurrentIteration"

rule.statement: Always re-fetch a work item after creating it via REST and validate required fields are set — ADO returns 200 OK on partial creation when a custom field value is invalid.
rule.rationale: Silent partial creation is ADO's most dangerous API behavior for agents. Re-fetching and validating catches the failure before downstream steps depend on corrupt state.

rule.statement: Rotate PATs via Azure Key Vault; never embed PAT values in agent system prompts, environment variables in code, or configuration files checked into source control.
rule.rationale: PATs have broad scopes. Embedded PATs are leaked via logs, process lists, and prompt injection. Key Vault provides rotation without code changes.

example.title: Pipeline integration with work items
example.code: # azure-pipelines.yml stages: - stage: UpdateWorkItem jobs: - job: LinkToBoard steps: - task: UpdateWorkItem@1 inputs: workItemId: $(Build.SourceVersionMessage) updateRule: "AddComment" comment: "Build $(Build.BuildNumber) completed"

section.name: agent-integration
section.p: Recommended subagents, prompt patterns, and failure modes for agent-driven Azure DevOps Boards operations.

list.item: ado-work-item-creator — converts spec.md or design.md into hierarchical work items with parent-child links via REST API.
ado-query-runner — executes WIQL queries, returns results as Markdown table for human review.
ado-board-auditor — checks WIP limit breaches, missing definition-of-done items, orphan tasks, and stale active work items.
ado-pipeline-bridge — links commits and PRs to work items via AB# convention; validates traceability on pre-merge check.
faion-feature-executor — already in repo; can drive ADO tasks once they exist.

example.title: Work-item creator prompt pattern
example.code: You are ado-work-item-creator. Inputs: spec.md (markdown), area_path (string), iteration_path (string), parent_epic_id (int|null). Output: JSON array of work-item objects with: { type, title, description (HTML — not Markdown), acceptance_criteria, story_points, parent_id } Use process Agile (Story, not PBI). Do not invent fields outside the Agile process schema. Iteration path separates levels with backslash: "Project\\Sprint 1"

antipattern.description: Writing Markdown in ADO description or comment fields from an agent.
antipattern.reason: ADO description accepts HTML, not Markdown. Agent-written Markdown displays as literal syntax or empty content. Convert to HTML using pandoc or a sanitizer before writing.

antipattern.description: Bulk-creating more than 50 work items per batch in parallel.
antipattern.reason: ADO throttles bulk creation; unthrottled parallel batches trigger 429s. Chunk in batches of 50 with exponential backoff and check for the Retry-After header.

antipattern.description: WIQL queries with dropped trailing brackets or misquoted field names.
antipattern.reason: WIQL is whitespace-sensitive and returns empty results (200 OK, zero items) for malformed queries. Always validate WIQL syntax against a known-good schema before executing.

antipattern.description: Using display names instead of identity SIDs in @mention comments.
antipattern.reason: ADO @mentions require the user identity SID, not the display name. Display names are non-unique and produce invalid mentions. Resolve via the Identities API first.

rule.statement: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints are mandatory at: work-item hierarchy approval before creation, bulk-transition approval, schema changes, and any pipeline configuration change.
rule.rationale: These operations affect all team members or the CI/CD pipeline simultaneously and are expensive to reverse.

template.purpose: Bash script to create a User Story work item via REST API with title, iteration, and area path.
User Story template with Gherkin acceptance criteria and dependency fields.
